Ad Text:
We are seeking a Financial Planning & Analysis Manager for a newly created role, a commercially astute leader who will set the benchmark for excellence in delivering actionable insights, driving performance improvement, and achieving strong commercial outcomes for the Group. This position offers the opportunity to make a lasting impact and define what 'good' looks like within our FP&A function with initially a very hands on approach, leading an experienced analyst to enhance our approach to business partnering and financial analysis.  

Reporting to the Deputy CFO this role will take ownership of FP&A function budgeting, forecasting and performance analysis for the Group. You're a CA or CPA qualified finance professional with significant leadership experience in FP&A with a commercial mindset. 

Key responsibilities include  

Lead and elevate the Group FP&A function across budgeting, forecasting, reporting and performance analysis 

Deliver insightful and strategic recommendations that influence business performance  

Drive cashflow forecasting/planning, working capital optimisation, and liquidity management with precision 

Own the preparation of board packs, executive reporting, and in-depth performance analysis 

Act as the key liaison for board-level financial queries and support the finance leadership in board communications. 

Lead finance system related projects and implementation programs to enhance capability and efficiency 

Strengthen governance, controls and documentation across models and reporting frameworks 

You’re a great candidate if you are 

CA or CPA qualified with strong leadership experience in FP&A 

Have a background in Retail or FMCG environments 

Hold advanced financial modelling capability, including scenario analysis, cash flow modelling and investment appraisal. 

Experience in Leading the implementation of ERP/major financial system projects  

A sharp analytical mindset, attention to detail and confidence working with large complex data sets 

Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills  

What’s on offer 

Direct exposure to senior finance leaders and the Board 

Flexible working arrangements 

Ongoing learning and development opportunities 

Generous team member discount 

Paid parental leave 

Employee Assistance Program 

Discounted health insurance 


Apply today if this role is for you.

A2B Australia is part of the ComfortDelGro Australia (CDC) Group, the country’s leading taxi network and a major provider of mobility, technology, and payment solutions.

Through our iconic brands 13cabs, Silver Service, Swan Taxis and Cabcharge we deliver end‑to‑end transport experiences, from booking and dispatch through to safe, reliable travel and seamless payment. Our services support corporate, community and disability customers nationwide.
Our network is Australia’s largest, with 7,000+ vehicles and 11,500 drivers, supported by contact centres, technology hubs and in‑field support teams. We power drivers, operators and passengers with dispatch, navigation, in‑car tech, apps and payment solutions across Australia and globally.
  
The A2B Way
We care about our customers, our business and each other. We work as one connected team, value accountability and collaboration, and encourage innovation to shape the future of mobility. We invest in wellbeing, safety, learning and development, recognise high performance, and celebrate diversity and inclusion.

The Role

We’re looking for a talented Insight Engineer to join our growing data team within a fast‑paced taxi and payments business. This role sits at the intersection of data engineering, analytics, and emerging AI, playing a critical part in turning complex data into trusted, actionable insights that support smarter business decisions.

Working closely with business stakeholders, analysts, and data scientists, you’ll design and deliver scalable data pipelines, translate business needs into robust data models, and enable advanced analytics and AI use cases. This is a hands‑on, high‑impact role where your work will directly influence how the business operates, grows, and innovates. 

What you’ll be doing

Design, build, and maintain scalable Azure data pipelines and platforms across Data Factory, Synapse, and Data Lake
Develop and optimise data models supporting data warehouse, big data, and Data Vault architectures
Deliver reliable batch and streaming data solutions, ensuring high data quality and performance
Translate business requirements into curated datasets and semantic layers that power reporting, analytics, and decision‑making
Partner closely with stakeholders to generate actionable insights from complex datasets such as payments, trips, and customer behaviour
Implement CI/CD and infrastructure‑as‑code practices to improve reliability, scalability, and cost efficiency
Support emerging AI and advanced analytics use cases, working with data scientists on feature engineering and model deployment (where applicable).
About you
 
We’re looking for an experienced Insight Engineer who has operated within enterprise‑scale organisations, managing complex data environments and working across multiple stakeholders. Our ideal candidate brings experience in data or analytics engineering, with strong hands‑on expertise across the Azure data stack and a solid grounding in data warehousing, data modelling, and ETL/ELT best practices. You’re comfortable working with high‑volume, business‑critical data, translating insight requirements into robust technical solutions, and partnering closely with the business to deliver reliable, scalable outcomes.

Skills and experience

Required 

5–7+ years experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or insight engineering roles  
Strong hands-on experience with Azure data stack:  
Azure Data Factory  
Azure Synapse Analytics  
Azure Data Lake
Solid understanding of:  
Data warehousing concepts (star schema, dimensional modelling)  
Big data processing frameworks  
Data Vault modelling 
Advanced proficiency in SQL, Stored procedure, PowerShell,  
Experience with real-time/streaming data (e.g., Kafka, Event Hubs)  
At least one programming language (Python preferred)  
Experience building and maintaining robust ETL/ELT pipelines  
Strong understanding of business data and ability to translate insight requirements into technical solutions  
Ready to apply?

If you’re an experienced Insight Engineer looking to work in a complex, enterprise environment and make a meaningful impact through data, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

Product Analyst - Optimisation Team Lead
Sydney office based, Mitchell Street, McMahons Point, NSW, 2060 
Team leader position 
Hybrid working model (2 days working from home & 3 days working in the office)

 

Compass Group is listed on the London Stock Exchange (Top 20) and is the global market leader in providing food and a range of support services to customers in the workplace, schools and colleges, hospitals, at leisure and in remote environments. We operate in over 50 countries, employ over 600,000 people, and are a recognized top 10 employer worldwide. In Australia, Compass Group Australia (CGA) generates $2.4Bn revenue per annum, servicing over 350 clients across over 700 sites, with 15,000 employees.

Join Compass Group and play a key role in shaping high-quality, sustainable food solutions across our diverse client portfolios. In this role, you will lead structured SKU and menu optimisation initiatives, combining culinary expertise with data-driven insight to deliver measurable value for our clients. Working closely with culinary, procurement, and sector stakeholders, you will translate complex analysis into practical recommendations that support smarter decision-making and long-term outcomes.
 
About the Role

As Product Analyst - Optimisation Team Lead, you will be a key team member of Central Culinary' team, plus a critical part in delivering incremental financial and non-financial benefits to member clients through data-driven analysis, culinary insight, and strong stakeholder engagement.

You will act as the key conduit between Procurement, Culinary teams, and Compass Group Australia sectors, supporting menu development, tender responses, and optimisation initiatives while leading a small national culinary services team.

Key Responsibilities
Perform net pricing, scenario, leakage, and inflation analysis. 
Lead end-to-end SKU optimisation programs, from opportunity identification through to consultation, approval, and delivery
Deliver structured reporting on optimisation performance, benefits realised, and initiatives in progress
Provide culinary and commercial expertise to support product rationalisation, optimisation, and alternate recommendations
Partner with procurement teams on optimised product specifications for tenders and sourcing activities
Work closely with sector culinary teams to develop and validate menus
Manage national stakeholder relationships with Sector GMs and Executive Directors
Lead and develop a team of culinary services professionals
Support business development activity with commercial data, modelling, and supplier collateral

 

What You’ll Bring

Essential:

Strong commercial and analytical capability with experience working with complex data
Sound understanding of pricing, costing, and commercial principles
Advanced Excel skills (financial modelling, pivots, lookups)
Clear, professional communication skills and strong stakeholder engagement capability
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver to timeframes

Desirable:

Experience in procurement, sourcing, contracting, and culinary environments
Exposure to FMCG, food, catering, or hospitality industries
Experience with tools such as Tableau, SQL, or Access
Relevant tertiary qualification (STEM, Finance, Economics, Supply Chain or similar)

 

Ready to make a meaningful impact while advancing your career? Apply now and be part of something bigger. You’ll collaborate with culinary and cross-functional stakeholders to interpret insights, share outcomes clearly, and support smarter, faster decision-making across product and menu optimisation. Join Compass Group’s Culinary team and help deliver sustainable, client-focused food solutions.

 

Working with a large global company means you can be assured that we have an attractive benefits program! This includes comprehensive learning & development offerings, a variety of recognition programs and company discounts, an excellent salary benchmarking matrix and at this management level also includes an incentive bonus scheme! You can read all about these here: Why work at Compass

We are committed to providing a recruitment process that is fair, equitable and accessible for all. If you require adjustments or alternative methods of communication in the recruitment process, please reach out to us on either talisson@compass-group.com.au or georgefouskarinis@compass-group.com.au

Initial 6-month contract + extensions
Lean Six Sigma Black Belt certification required
Location: CBD- Hybrid work model
Our client is looking for a Senior Process Analyst to join a large AI transformation initiative.

Working closely with business stakeholders, you will carry out 'As Is' analysis and identify how to optimise the process and implement a solution to uplift efficiency.  You will be involved in the design of the future target state, as well as the optimisation and uplift of productivity. You'll be supporting business outcomes, around how to reduce leakage and improve outcomes in the process.

Key Responsibilities:

Conduct ‘As Is analysis
Implement a business process management framework to systematically document processes including process mapping, defining key performance indicators and controls and identifying risks.
Essential skills and experience

Previous experience as a Process Analyst
Strong business process transformation experience
Must have Lean Six Sigma Black Belt certification (mandatory).
Experience delivering large-scale, enterprise-wide transformation
Ability to lead workshops and facilitate senior stakeholders
Work strategically (future-state design) AND hands-on (process mapping)
Experience within financial services is highly desirable
For more info contact Aparna on Aparna.sharma@peoplebank.com.au

This is a rare opportunity for a proven executive leader to step into a high-impact role driving large-scale programs across government, industry and research.
 
About This Role
This is a rare opportunity to step into a critical executive role with a national mandate, leading a portfolio that sits at the centre of government engagement, industry engagement across the energy and VET sectors, workforce strategy, and research.
The organisation is entering its next phase of maturity, and this role is pivotal in shaping how strategy is translated into coordinated, system-wide delivery across the entire portfolio.
You will take over a well-established function and be expected to build on strong foundations, bringing credibility, authority and a clear strategic voice to both internal and external stakeholders.
This is not just a relationship or advisory role - success will come from someone who can think at a systems level, communicate a clear vision, and demonstrate how they have personally led and delivered complex, national programs with measurable impact.
 
Why This Role Matters
This portfolio plays a central role in:

Influencing government policy and strengthening national relationships
Driving workforce planning, strategy and sector-wide insights
Integrating research, data and industry engagement into actionable outcomes
Lifting organisational capability and aligning delivery across a national footprint
Leading tripartite engagement across government, industry and unions to drive aligned outcomes
 As part of the executive team, you will help shape the organisation’s strategic direction, credibility and long-term impact.
 

Key Benefits

National executive role with direct influence on policy, workforce strategy and sector outcomes
Opportunity to lead a critical, high-visibility portfolio at the centre of government, industry and research
Work closely with the CEO, Executive and Board, contributing to enterprise-wide strategy and direction
Lead and develop a high-calibre, multi-disciplinary team, shaping capability at scale
Drive large-scale, nationally significant programs with measurable economic and workforce impact
Play a key role in the organisation’s next phase of maturity, structure and influence
 
Key Responsibilities

Lead a national portfolio, aligning government, energy and VET industry engagement, research and workforce initiatives
Drive strategic government engagement, strengthening policy influence and key relationships
Lead industry and union engagement, operating effectively within a tripartite model
Provide clear thought leadership, setting direction and articulating how outcomes will be delivered
Lead and uplift a senior leadership team, building capability and accountability across the function
Translate systems thinking into execution, connecting strategy to measurable results
Oversee workforce insights and research, ensuring outputs are rigorous, relevant and designed to influence policy and industry outcomes
Strengthen internal capability, ensuring consistent delivery across the full portfolio
Contribute to financial strategy and decision-making, linking investment to impact
Deliver high-quality submissions, briefings and strategic outputs for executive and Board audiences
 
What You’ll Bring

Proven experience leading enterprise-level, national programs or portfolios of significant scale and complexity
Background in federal/state government, national NFPs, universities or industry bodies delivering sector-wide impact
Demonstrated ability to operate as a systems leader, translating strategy into coordinated, multi-stream delivery
Highly developed executive communication skills, able to articulate vision, direction and outcomes with clarity and authority
Clear track record of owning and solving complex problems end-to-end, with tangible outcomes
Credibility and presence to influence senior stakeholders, including government, industry and union representatives
Strong financial and commercial acumen, with confidence contributing to investment and funding decisions
Experience leading senior, multi-disciplinary teams, with the ability to challenge, influence and uplift experienced leaders
A forward-thinking, strategic mindset, comfortable operating in ambiguity and driving change
 
This is a genuinely unique role suited to a proven executive leader already operating at, or ready to step into, General Manager level, with the ability to bring authority, structure and strategic clarity to a critical function.
 
If you think this could be the role for you then please apply now! Or, if you have any questions, please contact Aaron White on 0481 112 033.
 
At Fuse, we specialise in recruitment for strategic, operational and national positions across multiple industries. If you’re looking for a new opportunity, we’d love to hear from you!
Alternatively, if you know someone looking - refer them to us and we’ll give you $500 if we find them a new role.

What's the role?

As the Portfolio & Operations Manager, Enterprise Operations, you will play a critical role in driving the reporting rhythm and operational visibility across the organisation. Reporting to the Director of Enterprise Operations, you will be responsible for delivering data-driven insights that inform executive decision-making and track the health of key initiatives.

Acting as the engine room of the team, you will ensure leadership has clear, consistent, and accurate reporting, while also supporting broader strategic priorities across the enterprise portfolio.

*Note: Applications close 5th May 2026.

Responsibilities

Lead the development and delivery of weekly and monthly reporting for the ELT, providing clear and consistent operational updates

Generate data-driven insights on performance, organisational capacity, and change saturation to support decision-making

Partner with Finance, Strategy and Business Units to track benefits realisation and intervene where outcomes are off track

Support Portfolio Managers on strategic initiatives, providing analytical insights to monitor portfolio health and risks

Contribute to the implementation of enterprise delivery frameworks, including defining key metrics and governance standards

Ensure accuracy, clarity, and consistency across all reporting outputs and executive materials

Who are you?

Experienced in business operations, corporate finance, consulting or similar roles (typically 5+ years) within complex environments

Highly analytical, with strong ability to translate data into meaningful insights and actionable recommendations

Detail-oriented, with a focus on accuracy and high-quality output in fast-paced, high-governance environments

A clear and concise communicator, able to synthesise information into executive-ready reporting

Proactive and curious, with a strong desire to understand how different parts of the business operate and connect

Familiar with reporting and visualisation tools (e.g. Power BI, Tableau, advanced Excel), or keen to build capability in this space

Why News Corp Australia

Join a culture where innovation is our operating rhythm. You’ll work on a diverse portfolio of Australia’s most loved brands and play a pivotal role in leading the technological shift in how we create commercial content. Our vibrant, hybrid work environment and dynamic, values-driven culture empowers you to push boundaries, collaborate as one unified team, and make a positive, measurable impact for all Australians.

News Extras Benefits is a commitment to our people for their valued contribution to News, our audiences, clients and communities.

Our benefits include:

Exclusively ours - Access to publications and products from across the News Corp family

Perks & discounts - Exclusive offers across a range of products and services that deliver genuine cost savings and benefits

Health & wellbeing - Advice, support and facilities for healthy minds and bodies (including an onsite gym at our Adelaide, Brisbane & Sydney offices)

Time off - News offers a range of leave options to support your personal needs and moments that matter in life: Parental leave, Birthday leave, Volunteer leave, Career break and Compassionate leave.

Financial wellbeing - Support on making the most of your money

In addition to our perks and benefits, employees have access to a number of initiatives and programs that support their wellbeing and professional growth, such as:

Helping you grow - Access tailored development opportunities, including leadership programs, to cultivate your skills and support your career

Giving back - Make a difference to our communities through volunteer opportunities and company-organised charity drives

Workplace Inclusion and facilities – Engage with your passions through various employee-resource groups and accessibility to multi-faith quiet rooms & parents’ rooms

Who are we?

News Corp Australia is the nation’s most influential media organisation, operating more than 150 household-name brands across print, digital and multimedia. We lead the way in travel, fashion, food, health, homes and lifestyle, and we are the largest publisher across print and digital, reaching more than 16 million Australians each month.

The Opportunity

We're looking for a Senior Product Owner to join a high-performing digital servicing team delivering complex, enterprise-wide transformation within financial services.

This is not a greenfield or strategy role, this is execution at scale.

You'll be working on digitising some of the most complex customer and banker interactions in the organisation, including areas that require high levels of regulatory, operational, and technical detail (e.g. account authorities, lending processes, and servicing workflows).

If you enjoy solving messy, high-complexity problems and turning them into scalable digital solutions, this is the role.

What You'll Be Doing

Own end-to-end delivery of digital servicing features and initiatives
Translate complex business processes into clear, structured requirements
Work closely with engineering squads to deliver features from design through to production
Manage backlog, prioritisation, and delivery against committed milestones
Define use cases, test cases, and business rules for complex workflows
Drive digitisation of manual and paper-based processes
Collaborate with a wide stakeholder group including:
Business teams (banking, lending, operations)
Legal, risk, and compliance
Engineering and architecture
Contribute to AI-enabled solutions where applicable (e.g. workflow automation, document ingestion)
Participate actively in Agile ceremonies (SAFe environment)

What We're Looking For

Strong experience as a Product Owner or Senior Business Analyst in delivery environments
Proven track record delivering complex digital or transformation initiatives
Hands-on experience writing requirements, use cases, and managing delivery
Ability to work across multiple stakeholders and business units
Strong understanding of APIs, workflows, and system interactions
Comfortable operating in fast-paced, delivery-focused environments

Critical to Success

This is a hands-on delivery role - not strategy or advisory
Candidates must be able to "get on the tools" immediately
Experience in financial services / banking is strongly preferred
Backgrounds that work well:
Product Owner (delivery-focused)
Senior Business Analyst moving into PO
Digital transformation delivery roles

Nice to Have

Exposure to AI tools or automation in digital workflows
Experience digitising complex, regulated processes
Experience in large enterprise / banking environments

The Team & Environment

AI Governance Specialist

Sydney, NSW | Contract

The Opportunity

A leading NSW government organisation is seeking an experienced AI Governance Specialist to lead the development and implementation of enterprise-wide AI governance practices. This is a high-impact role focused on ensuring the safe, ethical, and compliant use of Artificial Intelligence across a large, complex environment.

You will play a critical role in shaping governance frameworks, embedding risk controls, and driving organisational adoption of AI policies-working closely with Legal, Privacy, Ethics, Procurement, and ICT teams.

This role is advisory and assurance-focused, influencing outcomes across the business. It does not involve building or delivering AI solutions.

Key Responsibilities
Develop and maintain AI governance frameworks, policies, and standards aligned to NSW and global best practices
Lead the AI risk management process, including system classification, risk assessments, and treatment oversight
Embed AI governance into procurement and contract management, including supplier obligations and evaluation criteria
Partner with Legal, Privacy, Ethics, and Procurement teams to develop AI-specific contractual clauses and guidance
Coordinate ethical and privacy reviews, ensuring outcomes are captured in governance artefacts
Establish and manage the organisation's AI system register and assurance processes
Monitor evolving AI regulations and standards, advising on impacts and required changes
Provide reporting, insights, and assurance to senior executives and governance forums
Lead AI change and adoption strategies, including training, communications, and capability uplift
Develop practical resources such as playbooks, FAQs, and micro-learning content
Deliver training on AI safety, ethics, privacy-by-design, and responsible use
Track and report on AI adoption, compliance, and governance maturity metrics
Act as a central point of contact for AI governance queries and escalation pathways
Build and maintain a network of AI champions across the organisation
Key Relationships

Internal:

Chief Data Officer & Data Governance Office
Legal, Privacy, Ethics, Procurement & ICT teams
Project Managers & Product Owners

External:

NSW Government stakeholders and assurance bodies
Vendors and suppliers (governance & compliance oversight)
About You

You are a senior governance professional with deep expertise in AI, data governance, and risk frameworks. You're comfortable operating in complex environments and influencing stakeholders at all levels.

Key Requirements:

10+ years' experience in Data and/or AI Governance
Proven experience implementing governance tools such as Collibra, IBM Watson, Credo AI, Informatica, or similar
Strong background in policy development, risk management, and procurement governance
Hands-on experience with AI risk management, including system classification and risk assessment frameworks
Strong knowledge of frameworks such as NSW AIAF, NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001, ISO/IEC 23894
Experience embedding ethical AI practices (bias mitigation, transparency, fairness)
Demonstrated ability to partner across Legal, Privacy, Ethics, Procurement, and Data teams
Excellent stakeholder engagement, communication, and influencing skills
Why Apply?
Shape AI governance at scale within a large, complex organisation
Work at the forefront of AI policy, ethics, and regulation
Influence executive decision-making and compliance outcomes
Drive meaningful change in how AI is safely adopted

Ad Text:
Program Change Analyst – Claims Transformation Program

6-Month Contract | Brisbane (Hybrid) | Large-Scale Transformation Program

Role Overview

We are seeking a capable Change Analyst to join a major enterprise-wide transformation program within a well-established Queensland organisation.

This role will operate at the program level, supporting multiple workstreams and providing critical visibility across change activities. It is a highly analytical and coordination-focused role, suited to someone who enjoys working with data, stakeholders, and structured artefacts to drive informed decision-making.

While aligned to change management, this role is less focused on end-to-end change delivery and more centred on analysis, reporting, and program coordination.

Key Responsibilities

Develop, maintain, and analyse program-level stakeholder registers (including SMEs and key stakeholders)

Consolidate inputs from multiple initiatives to provide centralised visibility of change impacts and activities

Support the development and management of communication plans, calendars, and intranet content

Conduct stakeholder and engagement analysis, providing insights and recommendations to support effective communication strategies

Assist with program reporting and governance artefacts

Support workshop coordination and logistics, particularly as the program moves into design phases

Work closely with the Change Lead to ensure alignment and consistency across program-level change activities

What We’re Looking For

Proven experience as a Change Analyst, Change Coordinator, or Project Coordinator within complex environments

Strong analytical capability, with the ability to interpret data and provide meaningful insights

Advanced skills in Excel and PowerPoint (essential)

Experience supporting large-scale transformation or program environments

Ability to work proactively and autonomously, with a strong attention to detail

Excellent organisational and coordination skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities

Strong communication skills, with the ability to support stakeholder engagement activities

Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little different. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions.

What you’ll be doing:

The Finance Manager, Group Financial Planning & Analysis role is to be responsible for supporting the FP&A leadership team to deliver on the strategic and financial goals of the group. This will be delivered by aligning the finance function with business activity, underpinning budgets and forecasts, through to the impact of cash flow, and the variable and fixed components of the cost base over the cycle. The role involves reporting and providing insights on a regular basis to identify forecast risks and performance.

This role is a 14 month, maximum term contract (you will still have access to annual leave, sick leave and our wonderful travel benefits). The position reports into the Head of Financial Planning & Analysis and this role has 2 direct reports.

Further responsibilities include:

Oversee the preparation and presentation of the group management accounts for Board, executive member and their leadership team discussions including supporting the executive in reviewing the financial performance of their leadership team’s departments
Oversee the preparation and provision of group quarterly reforecasts and any recurring structured budget development, ensuring accurate and appropriate use of key business performance metrics and relevant function owner support
Ownership of the Group’s Capital Planning Process, including management of the governance process and benefits tracking
Ownership and oversight as the senior business partner for the Corporate executives including Finance, Legal, Risk, People, Strategy and Corporate Affairs & Sustainability.
Support the Group Reporting Team for statutory reporting including, Impairment paper, Going Concern and CFO supporting notes
Provision of ACCC, Financier and Shareholder Quarterly reporting
Develop effective and influencing working relationships across all levels of management and with the wider FP&A team
Build and validate complex business cases in connection with business transformation, ensuring to justify potential investment and quantifying benefit, in support of the company’s growth strategy
Leadership of the Financial Planning and Analysis teams both your direct reports and the wider team
Assess and implement the most appropriate team structure, ensuring team leaders have the right leadership skills and subject matter capability for their respective functions
Build and maintain effective relationships within and across the business units & departments
Proactively work with internal stakeholders across the VA Group and external stakeholders, as required

You’ll be great in this role if you have:

CA/ CPA or equivalent qualifications
Tertiary qualifications in Business/ Economics/Commercial
Extensive experience in business partnering and finance support of business units or the wider Finance team
Experience within a large, publicly listed, corporate business
Extensive leadership experience
Extensive experience in developing forecast models and complex business cases
Development of KPIs and performance huddles for new business proposals
Proven track record in improving processes and delivering against deadlines
Experience in dealing with external stakeholders
Ability to think strategically and implement initiatives in a way that delivers business outcomes
Excellent communication skills and ability to provide concise accurate and professional reports and commentary
You are Brisbane based or willing to relocate to Brisbane.

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to):

Discounted flights for you and your loved ones (including $1000 worth of travel credits per year)
A flexible, hybrid work style. Work from home or the office, adapt your location to suit your style and needs
One of Brisbane’s most admired head office locations, with South Bank train station, a supermarket, pharmacy, and lunch options downstairs plus a 5-minute walk to the Busway, a rooftop restaurant and onsite gym (with a low membership fee) and South Bank Parklands just across the road
Discounts on travel insurance, lounge membership, car hire, accommodation, and experiences worldwide
Cheaper hospitality, retail, technology, beauty and wellness services
Free counselling and access to a wellbeing app to support your physical, mental, social and financial health
‘Dress for Your Day’ – enjoy the freedom to wear whatever makes you feel great for the type of work you do and the day you have ahead of you.

When everyone is included, everyone wins:

We’re all individuals. And we love that. That’s why we encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, our friends in the LGBTQI+ community, and anyone else who wants to join our family.

Ready to apply? We’re ready to hear from you. Apply now.

Company Description


Property intelligence is reshaping how the world understands the built environment, and Nearmap is driving that. We put powerful aerial imagery, AI-driven analytics, and geospatial tools into the hands of the people who plan, build, insure, and govern the places we all live and work. Our technology turns property uncertainty into decisive action, and our culture brings out the best in the people who build it.

Job Description


About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Commercial Analyst who excels across the disciplines of data, strategy and execution.

Reporting into the Director, GTM Finance (ANZ), this role is pivotal in connecting data-driven insights with commercial decision-making across the ANZ Go-to-Market (GTM) function. You will translate complex analysis into clear recommendations that shape strategy, enable operational efficiency and support execution at scale.

Working cross-functionally with key ANZ stakeholders — including Sales and Customer Success leadership — you will develop a deep understanding of the strategic, operational and financial drivers of the Australian business, research and analyse emerging trends and performance outcomes, identify improvement opportunities, and take a hands-on approach to validating and implementing solutions.

The focus of the role is to strengthen the analytical foundation of the ANZ GTM function, improving visibility of revenue performance drivers, enhancing performance reporting, and where applicable, supporting the wider Global Commercial Finance and RevOps teams in refining GTM systems and processes. You will play a critical role in shaping the operational landscape, supporting month-end insights, and ensuring our ANZ GTM teams are empowered with timely, accurate and actionable data.

What You'll Do

Revenue Reporting, Forecasting and Analysis

Attend and contribute to weekly ANZ forecast calls with Sales and Customer Success leaders, interpreting and sense-checking pipeline and revenue numbers.
Contribute to the weekly Flash Report and other regular reporting cycles.
Understand and interpret key ANZ metrics including new bookings, churn, downgrade, retention and price increase performance.
Together with the data team, design and maintain dashboards and visual reporting templates to surface financial performance trends, patterns and insights to support ANZ GTM leadership decision-making.
Partner with senior management to enable data-driven decisions, identifying data requirements across market analysis, planning, monitoring, financial modelling, pricing and reporting.
Support the Director, GTM Finance (ANZ) by contributing to budgeting, forecasting and reporting for consolidation.

Commission & Month-End Support

Provide ANZ-specific context and input into commission calculations and month-end processes, partnering with the global Commercial Finance team to ensure accuracy and local relevance.
Interpret ANZ-specific commission structures and edge cases, flagging issues and opportunities as they arise.

Strategic Analysis

Deliver analysis and insights on key strategic issues on an ad-hoc basis, including market intelligence, competitive landscape, product and pricing optimisation and growth opportunities.
Partner with GTM, Product and Finance teams to support strategic planning, investment decisions and commercial growth initiatives.
Build business cases and scenario models to assess new markets, partnerships and GTM strategies.

Stakeholder Engagement & Reporting

Own key stakeholder relationships with ANZ GTM leadership, supporting business case development and KPI reporting.
Deliver insights and recommendations that inform strategic and operational decision-making across the ANZ GTM organisation.

Process Evaluation & Improvement

Working alongside the RevOps team, assess, evaluate, and optimise business processes, anticipating requirements, identifying gaps and developing solutions that drive efficiency, accuracy and scalability.

Global Collaboration

Build and maintain strong relationships with global business partners, ensuring consistent understanding of data flows, inputs and outputs from multiple systems.
Act as a bridge between ANZ and global teams to standardise reporting and analytical processes.
Mentor and coach junior staff members within the team where applicable.

Qualifications


Experience

5+ years' experience in commercial finance, business analytics or commercial operations — ideally within a SaaS or technology business.
Go-to-market or commercial finance experience is highly regarded — familiarity with pipeline, renewals, commissions, bookings and churn metrics is a strong advantage.

Skills

Strong commercial acumen with deep analytical skills.
Lateral and critical thinking; ability to self-start and execute on non-process driven outcomes.
Advanced Excel skills with the ability to build and manage complex financial models.
Proven ability to influence decision-makers and present data-driven insights with clarity and impact.
Experience in revenue forecasting, financial analysis, and a solid understanding of KPIs and commercial drivers.
Hands-on experience with BI, data warehousing, statistical or reporting tools.
Experience with FP&A tools such as Adaptive, TM1 or Dynamics preferred.
Working knowledge of CRM systems; Salesforce experience is advantageous.
Familiarity with SQL, Tableau or similar analytics and visualisation platforms preferred.

Personal Attributes

Hands-on self-starter with strong analytical and conceptual thinking skills.
Comfortable operating outside of standardised procedures in a fast-moving environment.
Growth-oriented mindset with a drive to continuously improve and adapt.
Able to read a room — knows when something needs to be escalated and acts on it.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to present complex financial topics clearly to non-finance stakeholders.
Proven ability to manage competing priorities and deliver to deadlines.

Education

Relevant tertiary qualifications in business or a related field.
Financial qualification favourable but not essential.

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

*12 Month FTC*

SEEK's growth ambition extends beyond core job ads. The Adjacent Revenue Development (ARD) team validates and accelerates the growth strategies that turn SEEK's acquired and adjacent businesses into scalable, repeatable revenue, before handing off to SEEK core teams to continue to grow.

The New Product Commercialisation Manager – Adjacent Initiatives leads the commercial acceleration of Sidekicker, SEEK's flexible staffing marketplace, as it moves from Validation phase into Product Market Fit in FY27. You will own three interconnected growth plays: building a demand intelligence engine that surfaces Sidekicker at the moment of flex labour need; designing and scaling an outbound sales and marketing motion that improves CAC and ARPU against cold lead benchmarks; and scaling a SEEK Account Manager referral program that activates SEEK's deep hirer relationships as qualified pipeline.

You will report to the Head of Growth – Adjacent Initiatives, lead a squad of Growth Operations, Analytics and Growth Executives, and work cross-functionally across SEEK's Product, Marketing and Sales teams and Sidekicker's existing Growth team.
 

What You'll Be Accountable For

Validating and executing the acceleration strategy - Identify key commercial uncertainties, design experiments to resolve them quickly, and translate results into scalable growth playbooks, accountable for FY27 OKRs
Building the demand intelligence engine - Lead the discovery, design and implementation of a lead scoring model that turns data ‘signals’ into a qualified pipeline to execute against, and develop winning GTM playbooks to scale
Designing and scaling the SEEK Account Manager referral program - Design, enable and scale a referral program that qualifies SEEK hirers against Sidekicker's ICP, building a commercial motion replicable across future adjacent initiatives.
Leading your squad with commercial rigour - Direct Growth Operations, Analytics and Growth Executives to run disciplined experiments, with hypotheses, metrics and guardrails defined before you start and outcomes turned into repeatable plays.
Cross-functional orchestration - You are the threading leader that keeps SEEK Commercial, Product, and Marketing as well as Sidekicker's own teams aligned on priorities and progress, navigating the tension between moving fast in validation and building something SEEK core teams can eventually scale.
Qualifications

Essential qualifications, skills and experience

Significant experience personally owning revenue targets and delivering measurable commercial growth outcomes, you can point to specific results and explain exactly how you drove them
Extensive hands-on experience building and executing go-to-market models for products moving from validation to scale in tech, SaaS, management consulting, or a startup or new venture environment
Significant experience designing and implementing signals-based lead scoring or targeting models that turned data into a qualified, actionable pipeline
Extensive experience leading disciplined commercial experiments, defining hypotheses, success metrics and guardrails upfront, and translating results into repeatable growth playbooks
Demonstrated ability to build alignment and drive outcomes across complex, matrix or cross-functional environments where you held significant accountability without direct authority
Extensive experience presenting growth strategies and commercial recommendations to senior leadership, and facilitating structured planning sessions across diverse, cross-functional teams
Proven experience leading or managing a squad or team, directing analytical and operational resources to execute against commercial goals
Active, applied use of AI tools to accelerate growth analysis, surface hypotheses or scale experimentation

Do these behaviours/mindset expectations resonate with your skills & experience?

Commercially driven – You connect strategy to revenue outcomes, own your numbers and can point to the specific growth results you personally delivered and exactly how you drove them
Experiments-first thinker – You don't wait for perfect information, you identify the key uncertainty, design a fast experiment to resolve it and act decisively on what you learn
Analytically sharp – Equally at home in a dataset and in a room with an executive, you turn complex analysis into crisp, compelling recommendations that earn a green light
Low-ego collaborator – You build alignment through trust and sharp thinking in environments where almost everyone you need to move doesn't report to you
Clear and precise communicator – You hold a room, write recommendations that earn decisions and turn ambiguous briefs into clear plans others can execute against
Cross-functional orchestrator – You keep commercial, product and marketing teams aligned on priorities and progress, navigating the tension between moving fast and building something scalable
AI-curious and applied – You actively explore AI tools as a multiplier for growth, insight and execution — not as a shortcut, but paired with strong commercial judgement
Comfortable with ambiguity – You thrive where the playbook doesn't yet exist and are energised by the challenge of building it
Accountability mindset – You're accountable for outcomes you don't directly control and comfortable with that responsibility
Additional Information

At SEEK we offer:

Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
SEEK is committed to operating sustainably and is preparing for the impacts of climate change and the transition to a low-carbon future, and is working to minimise its environmental impacts which includes a long-term emissions reduction target of net zero by FY2050
Access to a wide range of discounts on things such as health insurance, fitness, food, travel, accommodation plus many more
Frequent events including sports days, annual Christmas party, hackathon, and trivia
Free kick-start breakfast every morning and fresh fruit available all day in our offices
Casual dress – every day

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

Graph Engineer

Location: Sydney CBD, 3 days per week on-site (Hybrid)

About the Role:
We are seeking a skilled engineer to support the development of a connected data platform that enables advanced analytics and intelligent automation across the business. This role focuses on building scalable data solutions that capture relationships between datasets and make them accessible for downstream use. You'll work closely with cross-functional teams to design, implement, and optimise data services that power key business initiatives.

About the Client:
Our client is a leading organisation investing heavily in modern data capabilities to drive innovation and efficiency. They foster a collaborative environment with a strong focus on technology-led transformation.

Key Responsibilities:
Design and build scalable data platforms that support relationship-driven datasets
Develop and maintain data ingestion pipelines integrating multiple structured and unstructured sources
Implement efficient query layers and APIs to enable flexible data access and analytics
Optimise data storage, indexing, and performance across large-scale datasets
Collaborate with architects and domain experts to align data models with business needs
Contribute to best practices around data modelling, governance, and platform design
Required Skills / Experience:
3-5+ years' experience in data engineering, backend engineering, or similar roles
Strong experience working with complex data models and non-relational data systems
Proficiency in Python and SQL for data processing and integration
Experience building and optimising data pipelines across multiple sources
Familiarity with modern DevOps practices and tools (e.g. Git, CI/CD pipelines, Terraform)
Ability to design performant data access patterns and query solutions
Nice to Have:
Exposure to graph or semantic data technologies
Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
Understanding of data modelling techniques for highly connected datasets
Experience supporting analytics, AI, or machine learning use cases


This is a great opportunity to work on cutting-edge data platforms where understanding and leveraging relationships within data is key to delivering business value.

12 Month Fixed Term Contract

Melbourne | Hybrid Working

The Role

You will work closely with Digital and Technology teams but remain firmly business-facing - ensuring the selected solution aligns with strategic objectives, regulatory requirements and stakeholder expectations.

This role requires a commercially astute operator who can confidently drive:

Business case development and approval

Executive-level stakeholder engagement
Governance and steering committee reporting



Key Responsibilities

Lead the full project lifecycle from concept through to delivery readiness

Drive RFP/RFT processes including vendor shortlisting and evaluation
Manage complex stakeholder groups including senior medical professionals and academics
Establish governance frameworks, reporting cadence and risk management processes
Ensure alignment between business, digital and external vendors

Key Selection Criteria

Ability to operate within regulated or services environments
Excellent executive communication and stakeholder management skills

12-month Fixed Term engagement

High-impact program with senior visibility
Hybrid working environment

Are you are looking for a role that moves you closer to commercial decision-making? Rather than being confined to month-end reporting or retrospective analysis, you will be in a position to influence how the business grows, invests and improves performance across APAC. This is the kind of opportunity that gives you meaningful exposure to senior stakeholders and the chance to build further depth in pricing, promotions, customer profitability, forecasting and strategic investment analysis.

You will be joining a business where finance is expected to add commercial value. That means your work will be used to shape decisions, challenge assumptions and support better outcomes across Sales, Marketing and Category. If you enjoy working in roles where your analysis has real traction and where you can see a clear link between your input and business results, this should stand out.

There is also genuine breadth in the role. You will have the chance to strengthen your experience across business partnering, modelling, planning and commercial performance analysis, while also contributing to areas such as new product activity, channel strategy, investment proposals and S&OP. This role would suit someone wanting to round out their commercial finance profile and build a stronger long-term CV. It offers the sort of variety and visibility that helps position you for broader leadership opportunities over time.

The environment will suit someone who likes pace, complexity and being part of conversations that matter. You will work with stakeholders across the business, helping bring financial clarity to decisions in a fast-moving consumer setting. There is enough structure to provide stability, but enough scope for you to make the role your own and add value beyond the obvious.

You will likely bring experience in commercial finance, FP&A or financial decision support, ideally from FMCG or manufacturing, along with strong modelling capability, advanced Excel and exposure to ERP and BI tools. CA or CPA is expected. Beyond that, the real fit will be someone who wants to step into a role where commercial judgement matters and where finance has a seat at the table.

If you are ready for a role that will stretch your commercial capability, broaden your stakeholder exposure and give you more influence over business performance, this is well worth a closer look. Apply now for a confidential discussion.

In this high-impact role, you won't just be following rules-you'll be building the blueprint for how a complex, large-scale organization adopts AI. You will bridge the gap between cutting-edge innovation and robust integrity, ensuring that as we move faster, we move safely.

As our Ai governance lead you'll develop and maintain the policies, risk controls and change strategies that define our AI journey.You will act as the master orchestrator between Legal, Privacy, Ethics, and Procurement, ensuring every AI initiative is backed by rigorous assurance and transparency.

Manage risk:Lead the classification of AI systems and oversee treatment plans to mitigate bias and ensure fairness.
Empower the organisation:Create the "Playbooks" and training programs that translate complex regulations into practical, everyday guidance for our teams.

Build a movement:Establish a network of AI Champions to reinforce a culture of responsible and effective AI use across the agency.

Hands on experience with Collibra, IBM Watson, Credo AI, or Informatica.

Excellent stakeholder management with a proven ability to partner with diverse functions
Strong ability to design adoption strategies that stick

Ad Text:
Principal Data Analyst

Contract for 12 months | Brisbane CBD | Hybrid working

We are partnering with a leading Queensland statutory authority to recruit experienced Principal Data Analyst. In this role, you will be responsible for providing high level analysis, reporting and advice to support management decision making on policy, programs and performance.

Key responsibilities:

Provide high level financial and operational modelling for business initiatives, programmes and projects including preparation of costings and forecasting models.

Work in a specialised environment, characterised by a regular requirement to modify and adapt established systems to deliver optimal outcomes; analyse a variety of options before choices can be made; and resolve problems within established data management systems.

Provide strategic advice, operational performance reporting, and analysis to support management to develop policy and programs.

Lead the development of statistical reports, including reports on clients, services and suppliers, employee performance targets, to improve planning and inform management decision-making.

You will also lead the preparation of statistical reports for external stakeholders as needed.

Provide high quality advice, guidance and reporting relating to individual and team income targets and time recording compliance.

Provide advice to senior management regarding the optimal configuration of business systems to support a wide range of reporting requirements.

What we’re looking for?

Proven ability to interpret complex data, identify core trends/variances, and draw accurate conclusions to support business objectives.

Deep knowledge of financial and budget management (Cash and Accrual) and a strong grasp of activity-based and unit costing.

Advanced skills in SQL and the Microsoft Suite, with high-level expertise in Excel and Fabric/Power BI.

Ideally, you have experience with Finance1, Aurion, Visualfiles, and the SQL Server stack (SSIS, SSAS, SSRS), along with SharePoint.

Ability to present clear, concise, and articulate rationale to senior management and negotiate persuasively.

Why apply through Lime Recruitment?

Enjoy two pay runs per week without contractor fees. As a Queensland-owned agency, we offer personalised service and are a Tier 1 supplier to the Queensland Government. Awarded Australia’s top-performing small agency in 2021 and 2022, with a finalist spot in 2023. Partner with Lime for genuine, experienced, friendly recruiters dedicated to securing the best opportunities for you. Company: Cognizant Technology Solutions Australia Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
Cognizant (NASDAQ: CTSH) is a leading provider of information technology, consulting, and business process services, dedicated to helping the world's leading companies build stronger businesses. Headquartered in Teaneck, New Jersey (U.S.), Cognizant combines a passion for client satisfaction, technology innovation, deep industry and business process expertise, and a global, collaborative workforce that embodies the future of work. Cognizant is a member of the NASDAQ-100, the S&P 500, the Forbes Global 2000, and the Fortune 500 and is ranked among the top performing and fastest growing companies in the world.

Our Culture

Your passion, integrity and experience are integral to Cognizant's success. You will join a dynamic and expanding global leader in IT and Business consultancy where you will be valued for who you are. Job Title: Data Analyst - Data Migration and Reporting
Ad Text:
Catholic Care Broken Bay (CCDBB), delivers a wide range of Social Services through its workforce of ~1000 staff. With the need for Social Services in Australia ever increasing, CCDBB has embarked on an ambitious multi-year enterprise wide Transformation, to lay the foundations for future growth, improve Client Care, improve staff working conditions, improve the ability to manage Risk and Compliance, and also launch new Services. As a result, this Transformation is in need of a Data Analyst, with a focus on Data Migration and Reporting.

The successful candidate will be a critical enabler of the CCDBB’s success, as part of a highly committed Transformation and Technology Team, and will get an opportunity to work across all lines of business, across an extremely diverse group of Social Services Programs, with exposure to SLT members and Senior Management. Working in the Not For Profit sector, the successful candidate will also feel the deep meaning of knowing their work contributes to helping CCDBB’s Clients who need care.

About the role
The Data Analyst – Data Migration and Reporting, is responsible for ensuring high‑quality, reliable, and strategically valuable data within the organisation’s Salesforce environment. This role has a strong focus on data migration from legacy systems, data modelling, and the development of insightful dashboards and reports using both Salesforce and Power BI. Working closely with Project Managers, Program Managers, technical teams, and business stakeholders, the Data Analys – Data Migration and Reporting plays a critical role in supporting successful system implementation, organisational reporting, and evidence‑based decision‑making.
  
Key Responsibilities:

Data Migration & Data Quality
Lead and execute end‑to‑end data migration activities from legacy systems into Salesforce.
Map, transform, cleanse, and validate data to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency.
Develop and maintain data dictionaries, migration templates, and data quality rules.
Conduct data audits and reconciliation to ensure migrated data meets business and compliance requirements.
Collaborate with technical teams to troubleshoot data issues and optimise migration processes.
Reporting, Dashboards & Data Visualisation
Design, build, and maintain Salesforce dashboards, reports, and analytics.
Develop Power BI dashboards that integrate Salesforce and other organisational datasets.
Translate complex datasets into clear, meaningful visual insights.
Ensure reporting outputs align with organisational KPIs and program outcomes.
Business Analysis & Stakeholder Engagement
Work with business units to understand data needs and reporting requirements.
Facilitate workshops, requirements gathering sessions, and data validation activities.
Provide subject matter expertise on Salesforce data structures and reporting capabilities.
Project & Program Management Alignment
Contribute to project planning, risk management, and milestone tracking.
Prepare documentation including data migration plans, test scripts, and progress reports.
Support Program Managers and Project Managers with data insights and impact assessments.
Ensure all data‑related activities align with project governance and quality standards.
Continuous Improvement & Data Governance
Identify opportunities to streamline data processes and improve data integrity.
Support the development of data governance frameworks and standards.
Provide training and guidance to staff on data literacy and reporting tools.
  
Key Capabilities & Skills:

Technical Skills

Proficiency in Salesforce (Reports & Dashboards, Data Loader, Data Import Wizard).
Demonstrated experience in data migration and data cleansing.
Advanced Power BI skills (data modelling, DAX, visualisation best practice).
Experience with ETL/ELT concepts and tools, consuming relational databases, and REST/SOAP API’s required.
Experience with Microsoft Fabric, or other Lakehouse platforms (Snowflake, Databricks) desired but not necessary.
Understanding of relational databases and data governance principles.
Analytical & Problem‑Solving Skills

Ability to interpret complex datasets and identify patterns, risks, and insights.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
Ability to troubleshoot data issues and propose practical solutions.
Project & Stakeholder Skills
Experience working within project or program management environments.
Ability to manage competing priorities and deliver to deadlines.
Strong communication skills with the ability to translate technical information.
Collaborative working style with the ability to influence stakeholders.
Qualifications & Experience

Tertiary qualification in Information Systems, Data Analytics, Business, or related field.
Experience in Salesforce data analysis, data migration, or CRM analytics.
Experience developing dashboards in Salesforce and Power BI.
Experience in structured project environments (Agile, PRINCE2, PMBOK).
Salesforce certifications (Administrator, Data Architect, Business Analyst) highly regarded.
Power BI certification desirable.
Experience in the Not For Profit sector desirable.
Key Outcomes for the Role

Successful migration of accurate, high‑quality data from legacy systems into Salesforce.
Reliable dashboards and reports that support decision‑making.
Strong alignment with project and program management disciplines.
Improved organisational data literacy and confidence in Salesforce.
Enhanced data governance, data quality, and reporting consistency.
Why join CatholicCare Broken Bay?

This is an opportunity to contribute to a significant transformation program within a purpose-driven organisation. You will work across a diverse range of services, gain exposure to senior leadership, and play a key role in strengthening data capability to support better outcomes for the community.
In addition to a competitive salary, we offer a range of benefits designed to support your wellbeing, flexibility and lifestyle:

Salary Packaging Benefits
Increase your take-home pay through not-for-profit salary packaging options, allowing you to pay for everyday expenses before tax.
Flexible Work Options (WFH)
Enjoy a healthy work-life balance with flexible working arrangements
Fitness Passport
Access a wide network of gyms, pools and fitness facilities at a discounted rate to support your health and wellbeing.
Additional Leave Benefits
Benefit from initiatives such as CatholicCare Day and other leave options that support rest, wellbeing and personal commitments.
Supportive & Collaborative Culture
Be part of a values-driven organisation where collaboration, respect and continuous improvement are at the core of how we work.
Professional Development Opportunities
Grow your career with access to learning, development and exposure to large-scale organisational transformation initiatives.
APPLY NOW

If you are looking to apply your data expertise in a role that delivers meaningful impact, we encourage you to apply.

About Us 
Automic Group brings together modern purpose-built technology with a dynamic and experienced team to tailor solutions for companies and funds to understand, engage and manage their investors. We provide a cloud-native single registry, employee share plan and board management platform, and professional services including company secretarial, ESG, accounting and finance, and investor and media relations. 

Today, over 1,400 ASX listed and unlisted clients trust Automic’s 250 employees to manage their registry, investor and board requirements – and Automic is ranked number one on the ASX by number of companies serviced, with 750 listed clients. In the past two financial years, Automic has won 50%of IPOs and 70% of listed companies who have switched registries. 

Our culture is innately entrepreneurial, comprising people who thrive in an atmosphere where ideas, actions and initiative are encouraged. Our core values, Customer Success, Ambition, Equality and Team are at the foundation of who we are and how we operate.

We are growing and are looking for an experienced Senior Technical Business Analyst to join our team in Sydney!


About the Role

This role requires an experienced BA who can independently own complex system changes end-to-end and who can work closely with business and technical stakeholders to deliver build-ready requirements as our tech grows.

Key Responsibilities include:
Operating in strict accordance with Automic’s Information Security Management System (ISMS), ensuring compliance with security, governance, and data protection standards.
Leading end-to-end business analysis activities across new features, enhancements, and strategic initiatives.
Collaborating with senior management, IT, Sales, Marketing, and Operations to gather, analyse, and prioritise business requirements.
Facilitating and driving requirements workshops, solution design sessions, and stakeholder discussions.
Working closely with developers and UX designers to refine and clarify requirements throughout the delivery lifecycle.
Translating high-value business priorities into clearly defined Minimum Viable Products (MVPs).
Supporting testing and validation activities to ensure delivered solutions meet business intent.
Identifying risks, dependencies, and impacts across systems and processes.
Continuously improving analysis practices, documentation standards, and tools to enhance productivity, quality, and security.

About you!

 

To be successful in this role, we are looking for someone with:
A bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Business, Finance, or related discipline.
At least 8+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst in enterprise software environments working within an Agile delivery framework
Demonstrated experience delivering across full software development life cycles.
Proven hands-on technical capability from functional requirements gathering to user stories & high-quality acceptance criteria & mapping multisystem impact analysis
Able to work independently owning delivery outcomes, escalating risk when required
Proven experience of leading structured workshops with key stakeholders able to manage up effectively to get the right outcomes
A systems level thinker, understanding cross system impacts
Experience within financial services is highly desirable, particularly within share/unit registry, managed funds, fund admin, wealth/ market infrastructure or investor lifecycle platforms
Why join Automic:

You’ll be part of a team where operational excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement are genuinely valued. This is an opportunity to deepen your expertise, contribute to meaningful outcomes, and grow your career within a high-performing, purpose-led fintech environment.

Key Benefits include:

Hybrid working
Professional career growth & development
Supportive & fast paced environment
Opportunity to grow with the business

If the above sounds like you, please apply for this exciting opportunity and be part of the growth journey with Automic!
